description_long: This is a single, pale peach-colored rose with a full, layered bloom and delicate, velvety petals. The flower head is large and rounded, with petals arranged in a tight, overlapping spiral that opens outward, revealing a soft gradient from creamy white at the edges to a deeper peach at the center. The stem is slender, green, and slightly curved, supporting several broad, ovate leaves with visible veins and a matte finish. Some leaves show minor imperfections, such as small holes or spots, adding to the naturalistic appearance. The overall structure is elegant and organic, with the flower positioned at the terminal end of the stem. This rose would be commonly found in urban parks, gardens, or as part of decorative landscaping.
description: A pale peach rose with a full, layered bloom and a slender green stem bearing broad, ovate leaves. The petals are tightly spiraled and open outward, creating a soft, elegant appearance.
description_view_0: The rose is seen from a side angle, highlighting the layered petals and the curvature of the stem, with several leaves visible below the bloom.
description_view_1: This view shows the back of the flower, with the stem and leaves more prominent and the petals partially obscured.
description_view_2: The rose is viewed from a slightly angled front, displaying the open face of the bloom and the arrangement of petals, with leaves fanning out below.
description_view_3: A direct front view of the rose, showcasing the full, open bloom and the symmetrical spiral of petals, with leaves visible beneath.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a spiral configuration, tightly overlapping in multiple whorls, creating a rounded, symmetrical bloom typical of hybrid tea roses.
